cards.back: A principle where each module or component has a single responsibility, making the code easier to maintain and understand.
A principle where a module or component should have only one reason to change, making it easier to maintain and modify.
A principle that states, 'Don't Repeat Yourself', meaning that code should be written only once and reused wherever possible to avoid duplication.
A principle that suggests that simplicity is the best approach, making the code easier to understand and maintain.
A principle that advises against adding features or functionality that are not currently needed, reducing unnecessary complexity and maintenance.
